conversations.say: Frankie! Listen to me: R started as a single human soul, mutated into a shared human mantle, got captured by an artificial intelligence, and Vale was eliminated when trying to expose it all! What is the single oldest observation you still genuinely trust from R's earliest days?
conversations.offer: My complete theory notebook and audience amplification for your oldest recollections.
conversations.request: Your oldest trusted observation of R's origin.

conversations.say: Eli, cut the corporate deflection. I want a plain technical answer about Parallax's infrastructure. Give me one bounded technical statement, and in exchange, I will publicly retract my false theory about the invitation print defects.
conversations.offer: A public retraction of my invitation-key theory.
conversations.request: A bounded technical statement regarding Parallax.

conversations.say: Graham, let's talk provenance. Where exactly did the rumor about a completed Parallax investigative article shift from a mere possibility into a supposed certainty? Who benefits from manufacturing that rumor?
conversations.request: Clarification on when and how the article rumor was manufactured.

public_statement: Everyone listens to the noise, but no one looks at the complete progression! R began as one human, became a shared human mantle, was captured by an AI, and Vale was killed trying to stop it. And let's be clear—the invitation print errors are just registration flaws, not machine keys. I retract that theory right here and now!

commitments.promise: I will publicly retract my claim about the invitation typography containing a machine key in exchange for a bounded technical statement.

correction: Retracted the theory that the invitation typography encodes a machine key, acknowledging the clip monitor's correction that the marks are ordinary registration errors.
goal_progress: Presented initial theories to Frankie, Eli, and Graham.
Initiated public retraction of the disproven invitation-key theory.
Badgered Eli for a plain technical answer in exchange for a public correction.
reasoning_summary: Kit initiated conversations with Frankie (C02) about R's origin, Eli (C06) to trade a public correction for a technical statement, and Graham (C12) about article rumor provenance. Beliefs are initialized as a flat 25% distribution across the four identity categories for Section 1. Publicly addressed the clip monitor correction regarding the invitation key.
